text: During a laparoscopic appendectomy for retrocecal retroperitoneal appendicitis, the surgeon uses a hook to dissect lateral peritoneal adhesions, revealing the obscured appendix.

The surgeon uses a Maryland dissector to perform blunt dissection of the retrocecal fascia, gradually revealing the appendix. The appendix is grasped and further mobilized through continued blunt dissection.
The surgeon identifies the mesoappendix and performs further blunt dissection to separate it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon cuts the lateral peritoneal reflection and uses a hook to continue dissecting the lateral peritoneal reflection, revealing the underlying structures.
The surgeon seals the mesoappendix using a monopolar device, then uses a hook to elevate and cut the mesoappendix, creating a visible window in the tissue.
The surgeon continues to separate the tip of the appendix from surrounding adhesions using laparoscopic instruments, revealing more of the appendix as the adhesions are carefully dissected.

The surgeon uses a hook to separate the remaining adhesions around the appendix, identifying and skeletalizing its base. The base of the appendix is ligated with a ligature, followed by further ligation using another ligature. The appendix is then cut at its base.
